articles.title: Big Shift In Retail Space As Institutional Capital Moves Into Laundromats
articles.narrative: The laundromat industry, traditionally fragmented and overlooked by institutional investors, is poised for a significant shift as sophisticated capital begins to enter the sector. This article argues that laundromats offer a compelling investment opportunity due to their resilient, non-discretionary demand, predictable cash flow, and long-term lease potential, mirroring the consolidation trends previously seen in self-storage, car washes, and gas stations. The author highlights the emergence of dedicated investment vehicles and a changing investor profile, with private equity and family offices now exploring laundromats, signaling a move towards professionalization and systematic portfolio construction. This shift presents new opportunities for retail landlords seeking stable tenants and investors looking for recession-resistant assets, provided the industry develops the necessary infrastructure for institutional-grade evaluation and deployment of capital.

articles.title: Law Firm Cole, Scott & Kissane Adds Second Dadeland Office in 21K-SF Lease
articles.narrative: Law firm Cole, Scott & Kissane has signed a 10-year lease for 21,000 square feet at One Datran office building in Dadeland, FL, marking an expansion for the firm which will retain its existing office nearby. The new space will accommodate the firm's growing team and enhance client services, with a planned opening in January after a buildout.

data_points.notable_features: 18-story office building adjacent to the Dadeland South Metrorail station, located between Palmetto Expressway and Route 1.
data_points.project_notes: The building was previously acquired for $150 million in 2016.

articles.title: Retailer Garage to Open 9K-SF Corner Store at RFR’s 160 Fifth Avenue
articles.narrative: Fashion retailer Garage has secured a 9,023-square-foot lease for its second New York City store at RFR Holding's 160 Fifth Avenue in the Flatiron District. The space, formerly part of a larger retail footprint, was divided to accommodate modern brand requirements. The ground floor asking rent was $500 per square foot, with the lower level at $150 per square foot.

data_points.address: 160 Fifth Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Historic building designed by R.H. Robertson with large showrooms and loft offices, featuring a restored facade and a new 3,300-square-foot rooftop terrace amenity.
data_points.project_notes: The retail space was previously occupied by Club Monaco and was vertically sliced into two shops to accommodate current brand requirements.

articles.title: CRE Industry Awaits Warsh’s Fed Policies After Senate Approval
articles.narrative: The commercial real estate industry is closely watching Kevin Warsh, following his Senate confirmation as the new Federal Reserve chair. Warsh's appointment comes as President Trump advocates for lower interest rates, a stance that could influence the Fed's monetary policy. Industry leaders anticipate Warsh will address issues critical to CRE finance, including interest rates, capital availability, and proposed bank capital requirements for CRE loans, aiming to foster a resilient and healthy finance system.

articles.title: Tech Firm VTEX Signs 6K-SF Lease at 286 Madison Avenue
articles.narrative: VTEX, a publicly traded tech platform, has signed a 10-year lease for 5,672 square feet at 286 Madison Avenue in Midtown Manhattan. The company will occupy the entire 14th floor, securing a high-quality, full-floor office space that provides a strong presence in the area. The asking rent for the space was $65 per square foot.

data_points.address: 286 Madison Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Located near Grand Central Terminal.

articles.title: Elevate Development Receives $116M in Financing for Student Housing Project Near Texas State University
articles.narrative: Elevate Development Partners, in partnership with Flintco, has secured $116 million in acquisition and construction financing for a new 759-bed student housing community near Texas State University in San Marcos. The project will feature 260 units across 280,124 square feet and is designed with a comprehensive suite of amenities to cater to student residents. Walker & Dunlop arranged the financing provided by S3 Capital, with Preiss slated to manage the property upon its completion.

data_points.notable_features: Amenities include a social lounge, bicycle room, package room, resort-style pool, yoga and wellness room, fitness center, study lounges, and conference rooms.

articles.title: CanTex Capital Sells Majority Interest in 1.3 MSF Dallas-Fort Worth Industrial Portfolio
articles.narrative: CanTex Capital has divested its majority interest in a 20-property shallow-bay industrial portfolio spanning 1.3 million square feet across the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. The buyer is a joint venture between Partners Group and a New York-based global investor. The portfolio features properties with clear heights ranging from 16 to 30 feet and a combination of dock-high and drive-in loading doors.

data_points.notable_features: Buildings feature 16- to 30-foot clear heights and a mix of dock-high and drive-in loading doors.
data_points.project_notes: The portfolio consists of 20 shallow-bay industrial properties.

companies_people.label: BUYER
companies_people.firm_name: global investor based in New York

articles.title: Kennedy Funding Provides $4.4M Loan for Refinancing of Residential Development Site in Spicewood, Texas
articles.narrative: Kennedy Funding has provided a $4.4 million loan for the refinancing of a 38.8-acre residential development site in Spicewood, Texas. The loan proceeds will be used by the undisclosed borrower to access capital for the next phase of the project, which consists of six adjoining parcels.

data_points.project_notes: The site consists of six adjoining parcels.

articles.title: Colliers Brokers Sale of 69,363 SF Industrial Building in Irving
articles.narrative: Colliers has brokered the sale of a 69,363-square-foot industrial building in Irving, Texas. The property, Building 1 within TriStar Business Park, was sold by Hopewell Development to GEO Plastics. GEO Plastics plans to occupy the building and create approximately 50 new jobs in the area.

articles.title: Partnership Agrees to Buy Church Land in Manhattan’s Upper West Side, Develop Mixed-Income Project
articles.narrative: A partnership between Rockefeller Group and Atlas Capital Group has reached an agreement to acquire church-owned land on Manhattan's Upper West Side from the Roman Catholic Church of Holy Name of Jesus and Saint Gregory the Great. The $96 million transaction will facilitate the development of a mixed-income housing project, with 25 percent of the residential units designated as affordable. The project will repurpose underutilized portions of the church campus, including former school and convent buildings, while the active church continues its operations.

data_points.address: 200 W. 97th St., New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: 25 percent of residences will be subject to income restrictions; includes retail space.
data_points.project_notes: Involves underutilized portions of a church campus, including former school, convent, and recreation buildings, while the active church continues its ministry.

companies_people.label: SELLER
companies_people.firm_name: Roman Catholic Church of Holy Name of Jesus and Saint Gregory the Great

articles.title: CBRE closes sale of 120-unit multifamily property in West Bend
articles.narrative: CBRE has facilitated the sale of Trail's Edge Apartments, a 120-unit multifamily property in West Bend, Wisconsin. Wisconsin Lakefront Property Management LLC acquired the asset from an ownership group that included its original developer, American Construction Services. The property, built in 2022, offers a strong amenity package and convenient access to local attractions.

data_points.address: 250 S. Forest Ave., West Bend, WI
data_points.notable_features: Amenities include a fitness center, community room, pet washing station, interior car wash bay, and keyless building access. Units feature quartz countertops. The property is located near restaurants, dog parks, and walking trails along the Milwaukee River.

articles.title: More investment sales in Cincinnati’s retail market? That’s what Marcus & Millichap predicts
articles.narrative: Marcus & Millichap's 2026 retail Investment Forecast predicts a busier year for investment sales in Cincinnati's retail market, driven by the strength of downtown Cincinnati, limited new retail supply, and attractive lower sales prices with higher cap rates. Despite a moderate recovery expected after negative net absorption and rent dips in 2025, the metro area's vacancy rate is projected to reach 6.5% by year-end 2026 due to new supply outpacing demand. However, the report highlights strong retail sectors in Butler County and Northern Kentucky, with increased capital expected for single-tenant acquisitions.
articles.market_intelligence: Downtown Cincinnati's multi-tenant segment has one of the metropolitan area's lowest vacancy and highest rent areas. The downtown area's construction pipeline accounts for less than 1% of new retail supply in the region. The Northern Kentucky submarket saw a year-over-year drop in vacancy of nearly 120 basis points. Cincinnati ranks among the lowest-priced major metros with high cap rates.

articles.title: All that new supply? It’s suppressing apartment rent growth
articles.narrative: The U.S. multifamily sector is experiencing sluggish rent growth due to a historic wave of new supply, particularly in Sun Belt and Mountain West markets, coupled with moderating demand. While national average advertised rents increased slightly in April to $1,758, they remain down 0.2% year-over-year, according to Yardi Matrix. Some previously struggling markets are showing signs of stabilization, but gateway and Midwest markets like New York City, San Francisco, and Chicago are outperforming the rest of the country in annual rent growth. The Twin Cities market, in particular, is projected for strong gains due to restrained new supply and solid demand.

articles.title: Senior Living Investment Brokerage closes sale of two-community skilled-nursing portfolio in Nebraska
articles.narrative: Senior Living Investment Brokerage facilitated the sale of a two-community skilled nursing portfolio in Bridgeport and Hemingford, Nebraska. The transaction involved a Denver-based owner/operator selling the properties to a Utah-based owner/operator, who acquired one community and assumed a leasehold interest with a purchase option on the other. This deal represents the second closing in a larger four-property disposition strategy for the seller, allowing the buyer to establish and expand its presence in Nebraska.

data_points.project_notes: This transaction represents the second closing within a larger four-property portfolio disposition strategy. The Hemingford community was structured as a lease-option transaction.

articles.title: Troutman Pepper Locke adds partner in Dallas office
articles.narrative: Troutman Pepper Locke has expanded its Real Estate Practice Group in Dallas by adding Kate Pennartz as a new partner. Pennartz, formerly with Holland & Knight, brings extensive experience in advising developers, investors, and lenders on various complex real estate transactions, including acquisition and construction loans, mezzanine financing, and joint venture structuring. This addition aims to bolster the firm's real estate capabilities in the active Dallas development market.

articles.title: Cushman hit with class action suit following data breach
articles.narrative: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Cushman & Wakefield following a data breach that reportedly compromised over 300,000 client accounts. The lawsuit, brought by a former commercial tenant, alleges that the brokerage failed to adequately protect sensitive personal information, leading to spam and scam communications for those affected. Cushman & Wakefield has dismissed the lawsuit as baseless, stating the data incident was limited in scope and that they are notifying impacted clients.

articles.title: Housing Notes: Appraisal Institute is being sued by experts in a dying field
articles.narrative: Jonathan Miller, a prominent real estate appraiser and market analyst, has partnered with The Real Deal to publish his "Housing Notes" column and quarterly New York City market report. Miller's column details the ongoing legal challenges faced by the Appraisal Institute (AI), the largest trade group in the appraisal industry. He highlights a pattern of alleged self-dealing and declining membership within AI, noting a 31% drop from 16,000 to 11,000 members between 2023 and 2025. Miller also criticizes The Appraisal Foundation (TAF) for its bureaucratic structure and high salaries, arguing that both organizations are failing the appraisal profession, which is experiencing a significant decline in new entrants and an aging workforce.

people.name: Alissa Akins
people.title: Former Director of Education and Publications

articles.title: Sam Sprei, former Brooklyn judge arrested
articles.narrative: A former Brooklyn judge, Edward Harold King, and real estate operator Sam Sprei have been arrested and charged with criminal wire fraud conspiracy. Prosecutors allege the pair diverted $6.5 million in investor escrow funds intended for a bankruptcy property sale in Freehold, New Jersey. The funds were supposedly for 'proof of liquidity' for a property purchase, but were instead allegedly siphoned off, with only a partial return to investors.

articles.title: Brookfield, Qatar  jump on trophy refinancing boom
articles.narrative: Brookfield and the Qatar Investment Authority are securing a $1.9 billion refinancing loan for their trophy office tower, Two Manhattan West, located in Hudson Yards. The 58-story, 2-million-square-foot property, which opened in 2023, is 97 percent leased with strong in-place rents. This refinancing is expected to generate a significant cash return of $273 million for the ownership, highlighting a trend of landlords leveraging high-quality office assets for substantial payouts.

data_points.address: 401 West 33rd Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: 58-story tower with amenities including a landscaped terrace, bike storage, conference centers, and a fitness and wellness center.
data_points.project_notes: Ownership is expected to pocket $273 million in cash returns from the refinancing deal. Average in-place gross rents are $132 per square foot.

articles.market_intelligence: Other notable refinancings in Manhattan include Soloviev Group securing a $1.8 billion loan for 9 West 57th Street, resulting in a $526 million cash payout, and Tishman Speyer and partners landing nearly $1 billion in payouts from refinancing The Spiral in Hudson Yards.

articles.title: Eli Karp wins reprieve in $15M guarantee fight tied to East Flatbush foreclosure
articles.narrative: A Brooklyn developer has secured a temporary reprieve in a legal battle over a $15 million personal guarantee tied to a foreclosed East Flatbush rental property. A judge vacated a prior judgment against the developer, allowing him to contest the deficiency judgment after he argued his former attorney failed to file opposition papers without his knowledge. The core of the dispute involves a significant discrepancy between two appraisals of the property, prepared by the same firm, which the developer alleges is part of a "loan-to-own" scheme by the lender.

data_points.address: 271 Lenox Road, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: The property was foreclosed on in 2024. A court ruled in 2025 that the developer was personally liable for approximately $15 million, representing the difference between the property's appraised value and the original loan amount.

articles.title: NYC’s top deals: Coney Island rehab center trades for $17M
articles.narrative: A drug and alcohol rehabilitation center in Coney Island, Brooklyn, was sold for $17.2 million. The 40,300-square-foot, four-story property at 2316 Surf Avenue was acquired by an LLC tied to Joel Basch from King David of Brooklyn, an entity associated with nursing home operator Robert Lichtschein. This transaction represents the highest-priced commercial deal recorded in New York City during the reporting period.

data_points.address: 2316 Surf Avenue, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Four-story building.
data_points.project_notes: Seller acquired the property in 1997.

articles.title: The Daily Dirt: Get Steve Roth some media training
articles.narrative: This article critiques the public communication strategies of Vornado's Steven Roth and Mayor Zohran Mamdani regarding New York City's fiscal policies and real estate development. The author argues that while development is a politically easier way to increase city revenue by attracting urban professionals, both Roth and Mamdani made missteps in their public statements. Roth's comparison of "tax the rich" to racial slurs overshadowed his positive remarks about the mayor, while Mamdani's pointed video targeting a wealthy individual was deemed reckless, especially in light of recent safety concerns among the city's affluent. The piece concludes that effective, non-reckless public messaging is crucial for both sides to achieve their goals and foster a mutually beneficial environment for the city's growth and real estate market.

articles.title: Judge orders $205M foreclosure on South Beach’s Goodtime Hotel
articles.narrative: A Miami-Dade Circuit Court judge has ordered a $204.7 million foreclosure on the Goodtime Hotel in South Beach, following a lawsuit filed by lender CIM Group against the hotel's ownership entity, Washington Squared Owner. The judgment includes the principal balance, interest, default interest, late fees, and attorney fees. An auction for the property is scheduled for July 1, unless the owner pays the full amount prior to that date. The hotel, completed in 2021, has faced financial distress and other allegations of unpaid bills.

data_points.notable_features: Seven-story hotel developed on a site acquired in 2015 for $36 million.
data_points.project_notes: CIM Group provided an initial $164 million loan in 2021, later reduced to $152 million. The owners and lender entered a forbearance agreement in 2023, which was subsequently amended multiple times. The owners allegedly stopped paying interest in 2024 and failed to pay off the debt at maturity.

articles.title: “Save Boca law” requires voter approval for future land deals
articles.narrative: The Boca Raton City Council unanimously approved an ordinance, known as the "Save Boca law," requiring voter approval for the sale, lease, or alienation of city-owned land larger than half an acre. This ordinance is a direct response to the overwhelming voter rejection of the One Boca megadevelopment in March, which was planned for city-owned land. The council also voted to propose incorporating this law into the city charter via an election before March of next year, reflecting a shift towards greater public accountability in land use decisions.

articles.title: “Underhanded”: Michael Stern’s JDS sues lender back over foreclosure of Mercedes-Benz site
articles.narrative: JDS Development Group is suing its lender, Cottonwood Management, alleging an "underhanded proceeding" related to the foreclosure of the Mercedes-Benz Places condo site in Miami's Brickell. JDS claims Cottonwood initiated foreclosure without proper notification and in violation of a non-disclosure agreement, causing significant damage to the stalled nearly 800-unit, two-tower development. The developer asserts the foreclosure has jeopardized its public benefits agreement with the city, deterred buyers, and impeded efforts to secure new construction financing for the $2 billion project.

data_points.notable_features: 67-story development with condos, amenities, hospitality, office, health and wellness space, hotel, retail, and parking.
data_points.project_notes: Project approval in 2020 hinged on the developer agreeing to build an $8 million firehouse for the city and contribute $5 million to public benefits, including a redesign of the adjacent Southside Park. The site was previously financed with a $43 million loan from Atalaya in 2020, refinanced in 2022 with an $86 million loan from Maxim Credit Group.

articles.title: Rishi Kapoor’s former right hand Daniel Motha faces criminal conspiracy charge
articles.narrative: Daniel Motha, former CFO of the defunct real estate development firm Location Ventures, has been charged with felony conspiracy for allegedly assisting Rishi Kapoor in concealing and diverting over $1.3 million in payroll taxes owed to the IRS. Motha, a former college friend and business partner of Kapoor, is accused of making false representations to investors and lenders, altering accounting software, and diverting corporate funds for bonuses and profit distributions instead of paying taxes. This criminal charge follows a civil lawsuit filed by the SEC last year, which Motha settled without admitting wrongdoing, regarding his alleged role in a $93 million real estate investment fraud scheme.

articles.title: Fort Lauderdale’s luxury sales are booming, but insiders are cautious
articles.narrative: Fort Lauderdale's luxury residential market is experiencing a resurgence in sales, driven by renewed buyer confidence and a strong boating culture. However, brokers and lenders remain cautious due to potential oversupply and a buyer demographic that is less inclined to pay ultra-luxury prices compared to neighboring Miami and Palm Beach. Despite a slowdown last year following tariff announcements, recent high-value transactions indicate a turning tide, with demand focused on new construction, turnkey waterfront homes that accommodate yachts.

people.name: Ben Koppenhoefer
people.title: Founder, yacht equipment and technology suppliers

articles.title: Inside the year-long Chicago standoff that ended in Zillow’s antitrust suit
articles.narrative: Zillow has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Midwest Real Estate Data (MRED), a Chicago-area MLS, and Compass, alleging that the two companies colluded to restrict Zillow's access to listing data. The lawsuit claims that MRED and Compass leveraged their market power to pressure Zillow into displaying listings that were initially marketed off-platform, threatening to cut off Zillow's access to a significant portion of Northern Illinois real estate listings. This legal action is the culmination of a year-long dispute over listing data control in Chicago, with Zillow asserting that rule changes and coordinated actions by MRED and Compass were designed to undermine its Listing Access Standards. The outcome of this case could significantly impact competitive strategies and alliances between brokerages and MLSs as technology continues to reshape the real estate industry.

articles.title: R.I.G. Capital snags O’Hare area apartment complex for record $167M deal
articles.narrative: R.I.G. Capital has acquired the 1,115-unit Pavilion Apartments complex near O'Hare International Airport from Brookfield Asset Management for $167 million. This transaction represents the largest multifamily sale in Chicago for 2026 to date. The property's strategic location on Chicago's Far Northwest side allows it to compete with both urban and suburban apartment complexes, a factor highlighted by the buyer as a rare opportunity given the asset's scale and prior capital investments by the seller.

data_points.address: 5441 North East River Road, Chicago, IL
data_points.notable_features: Located near O'Hare International Airport and the Park Ridge suburb, offering a competitive position against both urban and suburban apartment complexes.
data_points.project_notes: Average monthly rent was $1,573 as of 2023.

articles.title: Judge tosses OC Ventures’ bankruptcy, clearing path for Stephen Bus’ discounted UIC student housing deal
articles.narrative: A federal bankruptcy judge dismissed the Chapter 11 case of an affiliate of OC Ventures, clearing the path for Stephen Bus' firm, Up Campus Holdings, to acquire The Letterman, a 482-bed student housing property in Chicago's West Loop. The dismissal allows Up Campus Holdings to proceed with the forced sale of the property for $44 million, an $8 million discount from its initial offer. The ruling is a significant setback for OC Ventures' principal, Shangxuan Tan, who had sought to use bankruptcy to nullify the sale contract and find a higher bidder, but failed to provide sufficient evidence to the court.

data_points.project_notes: The property was initially offered for $52 million in April 2024, then reduced to $49 million in September 2024, $47 million in October 2024, and finally $44 million in April 2025. The bankruptcy filing was made in March 2026, hours after a state court ordered a receiver due to a $38 million Fannie Mae foreclosure suit. An additional $8 million in debt was provided by Monroe Capital and Daili Xiao, which triggered the foreclosure.

articles.title: Mansueto’s Chicago Fire inks McDonald’s for new stadium naming rights
articles.narrative: Chicago Fire FC, owned by Joe Mansueto, has secured McDonald's as the naming rights sponsor for its new $750 million privately funded stadium, to be known as "McDonald's Park." The Major League Soccer team will relocate to the new venue at The 78 development in Chicago in 2028, serving as a key anchor for Related Midwest's larger $8 billion project. The sponsorship deal with McDonald's is expected to run through 2040 and includes a 20,000-square-foot restaurant at the stadium.

data_points.notable_features: Privately funded stadium serving as the flagship anchor for the larger $8 billion The 78 development.
data_points.project_notes: The 62-acre riverfront site known as The 78 is being developed by Related Midwest. The stadium will open before the start of the 2028-2029 MLS season. The deal includes a 20,000 SF McDonald's restaurant.

articles.title: Texas Stock Exchange locks in Y’all Street lease
articles.narrative: The Texas Stock Exchange has confirmed its lease at the Bank of America Tower at Parkside in Dallas, establishing its new Texas Market Center. The space will include executive offices, a museum, and a broadcast studio, with a live stock ticker expected to be installed by Q1 2027. This move is part of Dallas's broader strategy to attract investment and jobs by offering a business-friendly environment with less regulation and no state income or corporate taxes, positioning the city as a growing financial hub.

data_points.property_name: Bank of America Tower at Parkside
data_points.notable_features: 30-story tower located near Klyde Warren Park in Uptown, part of a burgeoning financial district known as Y’all Street.
data_points.project_notes: The Texas Stock Exchange plans to launch trading in July, with its initial public offering set for 2027. Approximately $275 million has been earmarked for the exchange by a global group of investors.

articles.title: Related tweaks plans for delayed Austin office that would be firm’s first mass timber build
articles.narrative: Related Companies has revised its plans for a mass timber office project at 901 South Congress Avenue in Austin, reverting to a five-story design after previously expanding it to seven stories. The project, initially announced in 2022, was conceived as the developer's first mass timber construction, a method gaining popularity in Austin for its speed, cost-effectiveness, and sustainability. While the latest filing did not specify the square footage, earlier iterations included ground-floor retail and residential components, which may or may not be part of the current plan.

data_points.notable_features: Originally planned as a mass timber construction, a method known for speed, cost-effectiveness, and sustainability.
data_points.project_notes: Initial plans in 2022 called for a five-story, 107,000 SF project. Plans were revised in April 2023 to seven stories and 260,000 SF. The current filing reverts to a five-story design.

articles.title: National developer adds $44M multifamily project to stacked San Antonio pipeline
articles.narrative: NRP Group is expanding its San Antonio multifamily portfolio with a new $44 million project, NRP 301 Lofts, located at 13388 West Hwy 90. The development will feature 12 three-story apartment buildings totaling 382,486 square feet, along with a clubhouse, pool, and recreational areas. Construction is slated to begin in November 2026 and conclude in July 2028, further solidifying the developer's presence in the Texas market.

data_points.notable_features: Complex will include a clubhouse, pool, grilling area, park, and playground.
data_points.project_notes: The site currently belongs to the Schraeder Family Living Trust, which signed a utility service agreement in 2024 listing itself as the developer.

articles.title: Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin eyes Austin suburb of Hutto for $650M industrial megaproject
articles.narrative: Blue Origin, Jeff Bezos' aerospace company, is reportedly planning a massive industrial campus in Hutto, Texas, an Austin suburb. The project, referred to as "Project Blue Hub," is expected to encompass 1.3 million square feet for manufacturing, R&D, warehousing, and logistics. This development could bring over 2,000 jobs to Williamson County, with an estimated capital investment exceeding $650 million, potentially reaching $1 billion. The Hutto Economic Development Corporation has discussed potential financial incentives for the project, which would further establish Central Texas as an aerospace hub.

data_points.notable_features: Proposed manufacturing, research-and-development, warehouse, and logistics project.
data_points.project_notes: Expected to create over 2,000 jobs over five years with average annual salaries of $88,000. Blue Origin is reportedly deciding between Texas and Florida for this expansion.

articles.title: Quarterra offloads distressed Katy apartment complex
articles.narrative: Atlas Real Estate Partners has acquired The Maddox, a 326-unit apartment complex in Katy, TX, from Quarterra, a subsidiary of Lennar. The property was purchased at a significant discount to replacement cost, reflecting the current market conditions for distressed multifamily assets. Atlas plans to renovate the exterior and amenities of the 2019-built complex, anticipating that current tenant concessions will diminish as demand in the desirable Katy submarket continues to grow.

data_points.address: 1330 Park West Green Drive, Katy, TX
data_points.notable_features: Five-building, three-story complex with good school district strength and freeway access.
data_points.project_notes: Property was offering up to six weeks of free rent to new tenants at the time of sale.

articles.market_intelligence: Distressed multifamily properties have contributed to a surge in loans flagged for foreclosure, particularly for Houston apartments acquired in 2021 and 2022. Lennar recently sold its majority stake in Quarterra to TPG after Quarterra experienced losses due to rising construction costs and oversupply in the Sun Belt. KKR previously acquired over 5,200 units across 18 complexes from Quarterra for $2.1 billion.

articles.title: VC investor’s Pac Heights mansion sells above asking days after listing
articles.narrative: A modern mansion in San Francisco's Pacific Heights neighborhood, owned by retired venture capitalist Mark Perry and his wife Mauree Jane Perry, sold for $24 million, exceeding its asking price. The property, known as "the Perry House," was on the market for only a few weeks before closing. The buyer is an undisclosed entity, 2606 Jackson LLC. This transaction reflects a broader trend of rebounding luxury home sales in San Francisco, driven by affluent buyers, particularly those benefiting from the artificial intelligence sector.

data_points.notable_features: Sits on a double lot overlooking Alta Plaza Park and the San Francisco Bay.
data_points.project_notes: Listed for $22.5 million on April 13, 2026, and closed on May 8, 2026, for $1.5 million above the original asking price.

articles.title: Battery Club owners’ historic Oakland building slated for private school campus
articles.narrative: The Julia Morgan School for Girls is exploring a move into the former Bellevue Club building in Oakland, proposing to convert the historic 40,000-square-foot Beaux-Arts property into a private school campus. The building, previously acquired by the founders of The Battery social club with plans for a members-only establishment, was put up for sale last year after those ambitions did not materialize. This potential transaction highlights how Oakland's challenging commercial real estate market is creating opportunities for educational institutions to acquire discounted space.

data_points.notable_features: Historic 1929 Beaux-Arts building overlooking Lake Merritt, originally the Women's Athletic Club of Alameda County.
data_points.project_notes: The 2021 acquisition of the property by Michael and Xochi Birch for nearly $10 million was for a members-only social club, which did not proceed. The property was put up for sale last year.

articles.title: SF Decorator Showcase home in Pac Heights lists for first time since 1960s
articles.narrative: A historic Queen Anne-style mansion in San Francisco's Pacific Heights, originally built around 1900, has been listed for sale at $25 million. The 9,300-square-foot property, which served as this year's San Francisco Decorator Showcase home, is being sold by the widow of the late preservation architect Herbert McLaughlin, who acquired it in 1969 and spent decades restoring its original features. The listing comes amidst a strong luxury housing market in Pacific Heights, fueled by demand from the booming artificial intelligence sector.

data_points.notable_features: Queen Anne-style home with nine bedrooms, views of the Golden Gate Bridge and San Francisco Bay, restored original leaded-glass windows, and a south-facing Japanese garden.
data_points.project_notes: Property was purchased in 1969 for approximately $100,000 by Herbert McLaughlin and his then-wife. It operated as a rooming house from the 1920s through the 1960s before returning to single-family use.

articles.title: The German pension fund that lost $1B on a risky U.S. real estate bet doesn’t want to talk about it
articles.narrative: Germany's largest public pension fund, Bayerische Versorgungskammer (BVK), is embroiled in a legal dispute over its contentious exit from a U.S. real estate portfolio, including the Transamerica Pyramid in San Francisco. BVK is rejecting accusations in a lawsuit filed by Deutsche Finance America, which claims the pension fund stiffed it out of a $31.3 million early termination fee after selling the Transamerica Pyramid in March. Deutsche Finance America alleges that BVK paid its other partner, Michael Shvo, $79 million despite similar termination fee structures, raising questions about the deal's fairness. BVK, which invested nearly $2 billion into seven U.S. properties, estimates losses on these investments could exceed $1 billion and is attempting to distance itself from direct responsibility by citing a "fund-of-funds" investment structure.

data_points.address: Transamerica Pyramid, 600 Montgomery St, San Francisco, CA
data_points.project_notes: The sale of the Transamerica Pyramid and two adjacent properties resulted in a more than $200 million loss for BVK, part of a larger billion-dollar loss on its U.S. portfolio.

articles.title: W Magazine Signs Direct Lease at 1 World Trade Center
articles.narrative: W Magazine has signed a direct, long-term lease for its new headquarters at 1 World Trade Center in Lower Manhattan. The publication will occupy 12,456 square feet on the 46th floor, establishing a permanent, high-quality office environment to reflect its brand and host clients. This move signifies a broader trend of companies recommitting to long-term office spaces in New York City.

data_points.address: 285 Fulton St, New York City, NY
data_points.project_notes: W Magazine previously operated out of a sublease.

articles.title: Lendlease Opens Two-Building Habitat Apartment and Office Complex
articles.narrative: Lendlease and joint venture partner Aware Super have officially opened Habitat, a two-building mixed-use complex in Los Angeles. The development features a 12-story residential tower with 260 luxury apartments and a six-story, 260,000-square-foot creative office building, which also includes ground-floor retail space. The project emphasizes connectivity to the Southern California landscape through its design, incorporating full-height windows, private terraces, and an acre of open space.

data_points.address: 3411 S. La Cienega Blvd., Los Angeles, CA
data_points.notable_features: Features luxury apartments, creative offices, and restaurant-ready retail space with an acre of open space and design elements connecting to the Southern California landscape.
data_points.project_notes: The residential building is named Habitat Residences.

articles.title: St John’s U. Sells Staten Island Campus to Wagner College
articles.narrative: St. John's University has sold its Staten Island Campus to Wagner College, ensuring the property will continue to be used for higher education and community benefit. Savills represented St. John's in the transaction, which was described as a mission-driven sale requiring a balance of financial, institutional, and community considerations. The sale allows St. John's to reinvest in its main Queens campus and other global sites, while Wagner College plans to integrate the campus into its existing institutional footprint.

articles.title: Newly Built Pollo Campero Sells at Four-Year-Low Cap Rate
articles.narrative: A newly constructed Pollo Campero ground lease in Lake Elsinore, CA, was sold for $2.925 million, achieving the lowest cap rate for the brand nationwide in nearly four years. The transaction involved a private investor acquiring the property from developer Evergreen Devco, with brokers noting strong buyer interest and an all-cash 1031 exchange. The sale occurred before the anchor tenant, Stater Bros., began construction, highlighting the strength of the location and the tenant's appeal.

data_points.notable_features: Pad site to a new Stater Bros.-anchored retail development, located in the trade area's dominant regional retail corridor.
data_points.project_notes: Ground lease transaction.

companies_people.label: SELLER BROKER
companies_people.firm_name: Hanley Investment Group Real Estate Advisors

articles.title: Advance Realty Launches 1.1M-SF Life Science Campus in Bridgewater
articles.narrative: Advance Realty Investors has launched Continuum, a new 1.1 million-square-foot life science and advanced manufacturing campus in Bridgewater, NJ. The project, situated on over 60 acres, offers build-to-suit opportunities and is supported by an approved site plan and a long-term PILOT agreement with the township. The developer aims to create a connected ecosystem for research, development, manufacturing, and logistics, enhancing efficiency and collaboration for users.

data_points.notable_features: Purpose-built life science and advanced manufacturing campus offering build-to-suit opportunities.
data_points.project_notes: Supported by a long-term PILOT agreement with the Township of Bridgewater.

articles.title: Proprietary Data Obsession Is Holding Real Estate Back From AI
articles.narrative: Real estate firms are hesitant to fully embrace artificial intelligence due to concerns about proprietary data, despite the fact that large language models already access vast amounts of public and brokerage data. This 'data obsession' is hindering adoption, with only 5% of professionals trusting AI enough for critical decisions, according to a survey. Experts suggest that companies should focus on becoming 'data-smart' rather than 'data-secretive,' as employees are often using 'shadow AI' with company data anyway. To mitigate risks and leverage AI effectively, firms are advised to invest in company-wide systems, establish clear data usage contracts with third-party developers, and foster internal AI champions through training and collaborative initiatives.

articles.title: Compass Datacenters CEO On Walking Away From 12M SF Project: 'We'll Go To Another Spot'
articles.narrative: Compass Datacenters has withdrawn from a massive 11.6M SF data center development in Prince William County, Virginia, after a rezoning approval was overturned and the county signaled its opposition to the project. CEO Chris Crosby stated the company spent $40M pursuing the project but will now seek other locations, emphasizing that the U.S. is large enough to find communities that welcome data center investment. The decision follows a legal challenge by preservation groups and a shift in local political sentiment against data center development near the Manassas National Battlefield Park.

data_points.notable_features: Planned near the historic Manassas National Battlefield Park.
data_points.project_notes: Compass Datacenters spent $40M pursuing the project before withdrawing. The rezoning was struck down on a technicality related to public meeting notice requirements. QTS, a co-developer, is appealing the ruling independently.

articles.title: Construction Costs Rise 6.2% Through April
articles.narrative: Construction input prices in the U.S. have risen 6.2% since January, with April seeing a 1.7% increase over March, according to an analysis by Associated Builders and Contractors based on Bureau of Labor Statistics data. This surge, driven by inflation, tariffs, and geopolitical factors, marks a more significant increase in the first four months of 2026 than in the preceding three years combined. Energy costs, particularly crude petroleum, natural gas, and unprocessed energy materials, are the primary drivers of these price hikes, alongside notable increases in softwood lumber and steel products.

articles.title: Google, SpaceX In Talks To Launch Orbital Data Centers As Space Race Accelerates
articles.narrative: Google and SpaceX are reportedly in discussions for a rocket-launch deal to deploy Google's Project Suncatcher, an AI data center in low Earth orbit. This potential partnership highlights the accelerating race to establish space-based AI infrastructure, driven by the promise of uninterrupted solar power. The emerging sector faces significant challenges, primarily high launch costs and limited rocket availability, which companies like Cowboy Space and Blue Origin are also attempting to address through integrated launch and data center systems or large satellite constellations. NASA's engagement with these proposals, despite raising concerns, indicates the agency views orbital data centers as a credible near-term possibility.
